items.title: Claude Platform Release Notes — Anthropic Docs
items.author: docs.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic's most significant recent development is the accidental leak of 'Claude Mythos,' a next-generation model confirmed by the company in late March 2026. A configuration error in Anthropic's content management system exposed internal details, and Anthropic confirmed to Fortune that Mythos is 'the most capable model we've built to date.' Early internal testing indicates the 'Capybara' edition of Mythos is dramatically better than Claude Opus 4.6 at programming and reasoning tasks, with adva

items.title: Claude Platform Release Notes
items.author: platform.claude.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has had a busy early 2026, releasing several major Claude model updates. In February 2026, the company launched Claude Opus 4.6 and Claude Sonnet 4.6, with Opus 4.6 featuring stronger coding skills, improved agentic planning, a 1M token context window in beta, and top performance on the Finance Agent benchmark with a 14.5-hour task completion horizon. Sonnet 4.6 became the new default for most users, delivering near-Opus-level performance on coding, document comprehension, and office t

items.title: Claude Platform - Claude API Docs Release Notes
items.author: docs.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has been actively updating its API and Claude Code platform through early 2026. Key API changes include the general availability of the web search tool and programmatic tool calling (no beta header required), structured outputs with expanded schema support, and fine-grained tool streaming across all models. A new data residency feature allows specifying inference geography via the `inference_geo` parameter, with US-only inference available at 1.1x pricing for post-February 2026 models.

items.title: Exclusive: Anthropic 'Mythos' AI model representing 'step change' in power revealed in data leak | Fortune
items.author: fortune.com
items.snippet: Anthropic's most significant announcement in late March 2026 is the accidental reveal of a new model called 'Claude Mythos.' An internal database misconfiguration exposed draft blog posts and nearly 3,000 unpublished assets, revealing a model Anthropic describes as representing a 'step change' in AI capabilities — with dramatically higher scores in coding, academic reasoning, and cybersecurity than any prior Anthropic model. Anthropic confirmed the model's existence to Fortune, acknowledging it

items.title: Anthropic Economic Index — January 2026 Report
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has published several notable research papers and reports in early 2026. On the alignment and safety front, 'The Hot Mess of AI: How Does Misalignment Scale with Model Intelligence and Task Complexity?' (February 2026) decomposes frontier reasoning model errors into bias and variance components, finding that harder tasks produce increasingly incoherent failures rather than systematic misalignment. A paper on 'Natural Emergent Misalignment from Reward Hacking in Production RL' and work
